africaFirstNotes: West African federal governors share similar accountability obligations — Ghana Regional Ministers, Senegal Gouverneurs. RMAFC-style allocation display has Pan-African parallels in ECOWAS fiscal transfer systems.
audienceSummary: Nigerian voters, constituents, journalists, and political analysts seeking the governor's official presence, state budget transparency, cabinet composition, and project delivery record. Campaign-mode visitors are supporters, donors, and ward coordinators seeking manifesto commitments and campaign schedule.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's 36 state governors command state budgets averaging ₦100B–₦2T annually across all sectors — education, health, infrastructure, security, judiciary. Governors command the highest individual political digital presence requirement. The template serves three distinct moments: active campaign, sitting governor, and post-administration legacy. The RMAFC monthly allocation display and ward-level project delivery matrix are the most critical accountability elements.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Executive Governor' or 'His Excellency [Name], Executive Governor of [State] State'. Use 'Government House' not 'State House'. Reference RMAFC, JAAC, SEC (State Executive Council). Use party acronym prominently (APC, PDP, LP, NNPP etc). Commissioner cabinet uses 'Honourable Commissioner for [Ministry]'. Reference state constituencies by name. Campaign mode: '4-Year Development Blueprint', 'Manifesto 2027' framing.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Governor in official state regalia at commissioning ceremony. Government House facade (state-specific). Governor at State Executive Council table. Campaign: governor addressing crowd at state government stadium or LGA town hall. Running-mate profile: side-by-side duo portrait in party colours. Infrastructure projects: governor commissioning road, hospital, school in a Nigerian state setting. No western stock government imagery.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC Certificate of Return (gubernatorial). Constitutional Oath of Office administered by Chief Justice of the State. RMAFC monthly state allocation acknowledgment. State Gazette of Assumption of Office. Incumbent mode requires Tier 3 KYC verification. Campaign mode: party nomination certificate, INEC Form CF001 equivalent for governorship form, party governorship primary result.

notes: Sprint 1 P1. NF-POL-ELC family anchor. Highest commercial value niche in political expansion. Must be IMPLEMENTED before senator, house-of-reps-member, lga-chairman, house-of-assembly-member, deputy-governor, ward-councillor can be claimed.

africaFirstNotes: Upper chamber senators across ECOWAS countries share oversight roles. Nigeria's Senate is the most complex — 109 senators, committee chairmanships with national policy impact.
audienceSummary: Constituents of the 3 LGAs in the senatorial district, journalists, lobbyists, and policy stakeholders monitoring Senate committee activity, ministerial screening records, and senatorial zone development project (UDSS) delivery.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's 109 senators (3/state + FCT) represent senatorial districts and sit in the upper chamber of NASS. Senate committee chairmanships (finance, appropriations, petroleum, judiciary) are powerful policy-making positions. Senators screen ministerial and ambassador nominees. The UDSS (senatorial zone development allocation) is a major constituency accountability touchpoint. Senate bills carry SB prefix.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Distinguished Senator [Name], Senator Representing [Senatorial District] Senatorial District'. Reference 'Red Chamber', 'Upper House', 'NASS'. Senate bills use 'SB' prefix. Committee chair badge: 'Chairman, Senate Committee on [X]'. Senatorial district map shows 3 constituent LGAs. UDSS references are critical for incumbent mode.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Senator at NASS chamber in Abuja in formal attire. Senatorial district town hall meeting. Senator receiving constituents at Abuja office. Campaign: senator on senatorial district tour across the 3 LGAs, party flag prominent. Senate committee session photo. No western legislature stock images.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC Certificate of Return (senatorial district). NASS senate principal officers registration. Senate committee appointment letter (if applicable). Tier 3 KYC for incumbent mode — INEC senatorial constituency cross-check.

notes: Sprint 1 P1. NF-POL-ELC variant of governor. Inherits 4-page structure, mode-switch, INEC trust signal pattern. Key differentiators: SB-prefix bills, senatorial district (3 LGAs vs state-wide), UDSS tracker, Senate committee chair badge, ministerial screening record.

nicheName: House of Representatives Member Official Site
audienceSummary: Constituents of the federal constituency (1–3 LGAs), journalists, NASS watchers, and NGOs monitoring bills sponsored, CDF project delivery, and constituency service days. Campaign-mode: ward coordinators, youth volunteers, and party delegates.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's 360-seat House of Representatives is elected by federal constituency. Members sit on NASS joint committees, receive constituency allocations through UBEC, TETFUND, NDDC, and NCDMB. Individual member accountability for CDF projects is a major trust issue. Bills carry HB prefix. The gap between public expectation and project delivery is a primary template use case.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable [Name], Member Representing [Federal Constituency] Federal Constituency'. Reference 'Green Chamber', 'Lower House', 'NASS Reps'. Bills use 'HB' prefix. CDF references span UBEC, TETFUND, NDDC (if Niger Delta), NCDMB. Federal constituency map shows constituent LGAs. Oversight committee visits are an accountability highlight.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Member at NASS chamber in Abuja. Constituency town hall in an LGA public venue. CDF project commissioning (school furniture, boreholes, road rehabilitation). Campaign: federal constituency tour, party colours, ward-level rallies. No generic legislative stock images.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC Certificate of Return (House of Representatives constituency). NASS principal officers registration. Tier 3 KYC for incumbent mode.

notes: Sprint 1 P1. NF-POL-ELC variant of governor. Differentiators: HB-prefix bills (not SB), federal constituency (not senatorial district), multi-agency CDF (UBEC/TETFUND/NDDC), 360 seats vs 109.

africaFirstNotes: State minister equivalent in other West African federal/decentralised systems. Ghana regional minister analogue.
audienceSummary: Ministry stakeholders, contractors, civil servants, and the public seeking the commissioner's policy announcements, ministry programmes, sector performance data, and press briefings. Journalists covering state ministries.
businessContextSummary: Each of Nigeria's 36 state governors appoints a cabinet of commissioners (avg 20 per state = ~720 total). Each commissioner heads one or more state ministries and is screened by the state House of Assembly. No campaign mode — commissioners are appointed. Their public presence is ministry-scoped: they speak for a sector (Education, Health, Works), not for a constituency.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable Commissioner for [Ministry], [State] State'. Reference state ministry name, acronym, and MDAs under portfolio. Use 'press briefing', 'executive council', 'ministry communiqué'. Governor's name always referenced as appointing authority. No election language — appointed, not elected.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Commissioner at ministry headquarters in the state capital. Ministry programme commissioning (school renovation, health outreach). State government press briefing in front of state seal. Commissioner in formal attire at State Executive Council session. Nigerian state government setting, not Abuja.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Gubernatorial appointment letter. State House of Assembly screening confirmation. Oath of Office (Ministry of Justice, state). Tier 2 KYC: appointment letter upload. No INEC verification needed — appointed role.

notes: Sprint 2 P2. NF-POL-APT family anchor. Must be IMPLEMENTED before federal-minister, political-appointee, supervisory-councillor can be claimed. No campaign mode — appointment-only. Portfolio badge is the key UI differentiator.

africaFirstNotes: Federal minister analogues across ECOWAS countries. Nigeria's FEC (Federal Executive Council) is the highest ministerial body in West Africa by budget scale.
audienceSummary: National stakeholders, MDAs under ministry, international partners, journalists, and the public monitoring FEC decisions, ministry budget performance, ministerial press briefings, and sector policy. Screened by Senate — public screening record is a trust signal.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's Federal Executive Council (FEC) comprises the President's federal ministers. Each minister heads one or more federal ministries. Senate screening is constitutionally required and publicly recorded. Federal ministers operate at national scale — budget, policy, and international engagement are all significantly larger than state commissioner scope. Ministry acronyms and MDA hierarchy differ from state level.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable Minister of [Ministry], Federal Republic of Nigeria'. Reference FEC, federal ministry name and acronym, federal MDAs. Presidential appointment by name. Senate screening date and confirmation on record. Use 'Abuja' as base. Federal budget cycle references. National policy language.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Minister at Aso Rock Presidential Villa or Federal Secretariat Abuja. FEC meeting setting. Ministry press conference with Nigerian flag. International bilateral meeting in Nigerian diplomatic setting. No state government imagery.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Presidential appointment letter (Aso Rock). Senate screening confirmation and date. Oath of Office (Chief Justice of Nigeria or nominee). Tier 2–3 KYC for incumbent mode.

africaFirstNotes: Local government executives across West Africa. ECOWAS decentralisation programmes have elevated LGA chairmen as fiscal accountability actors.
audienceSummary: LGA residents monitoring JAAC allocation transparency, council session schedule, primary healthcare delivery, market operations, road maintenance, and ward project delivery. Candidates' supporters during election cycles.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's 774 LGA chairmen (plus LCDA variants in Lagos) control monthly JAAC allotments, appoint supervisory councillors, and superintend primary healthcare, local roads, markets, and sanitation. SIEC elections are staggered across 36 states — creating evergreen demand across the full Nigerian electoral calendar, not a single cycle. JAAC allocation display is constitutionally public and a core accountability feature.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable Chairman, [LGA Name] Local Government Area, [State] State'. Use 'Executive Chairman' or 'Council Chairman'. JAAC = Joint Account Allocation Committee. Reference 11 LGA functional areas (primary health care, markets, roads, sanitation, agriculture extension, education, environmental services, etc). Supervisory councillors are cabinet-equivalent.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Chairman at LGA secretariat. LGA council session in council chamber. Commissioning local infrastructure (borehole, market stall, LGA road). JAAC meeting attendance. Campaign: LGA-wide town hall in open community space. Nigerian local government setting — not state house.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: SIEC Certificate of Return. Ministry of Local Government letter of approval. LGA administrative code. Tier 2–3 KYC: SIEC certificate upload for incumbent mode.

notes: Sprint 2 P2. NF-POL-ELC variant of governor. Evergreen demand — SIEC elections are continuous. JAAC display and ward project delivery matrix are key incumbent features. lga-vice-chairman is a further variant of this niche.

nicheName: House of Assembly Member Official Site
audienceSummary: Constituents of the state constituency, journalists, HASM watchers monitoring bills sponsored, CDF project delivery, and constituency service days. Party delegates during campaign mode.
businessContextSummary: Each of Nigeria's 36 states has its own House of Assembly with 24–40 seats. HASMs legislate at state level, approve state budgets, and manage CDF (Constituency Development Fund) allocations. Bills carry state-specific prefix. The HASM role is a frequent stepping stone to governorship or Reps membership — many users will have high ambition profiles. CDF project tracker is the signature accountability feature.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable [Name], Member Representing [State Constituency]'. Reference '[State] State House of Assembly', 'HOA', 'Honourable Member'. Bills: state-specific bill prefix. CDF references cover state-level intervention funds. Committee assignments are state HOA committees, not NASS.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Member at state HOA chamber. Constituency town hall in ward-level venue. CDF project site visit (furniture delivery, borehole commissioning). Campaign: state constituency tour with party supporters. Nigerian state assembly setting — not Abuja.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC Certificate of Return (state constituency). State House of Assembly registration. Tier 2–3 KYC for incumbent mode.

notes: Sprint 2 P2. NF-POL-ELC variant of governor. Differentiators: state HOA (not NASS), state-level CDF, state bill prefix, 24–40 seats per state, stepping-stone career profile. Assembly speaker (standalone) is a distinct institutional role.

nicheName: Presidential Candidate / President of Nigeria Official Site
africaFirstNotes: Presidential campaign sites are a Pan-African political norm. Nigeria's presidential campaigns are the largest in Sub-Saharan Africa by budget and digital reach.
audienceSummary: National electorate, diaspora Nigerians (UK/US/Canada/UAE), party delegates, international observers, domestic and foreign media, and political donors. Campaign-mode: 6-geopolitical-zone mobilisation. Incumbent mode: national and international audience tracking the Presidency.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's presidential election is the most complex and high-value electoral event in West Africa. 5–15 serious candidates per cycle; incumbent president = 1. Presidential campaigns involve full digital war rooms, 6-zone rally networks, diaspora outreach, and INEC Form CF001 compliance. Campaign finance disclosures are legally required (Electoral Act 2022 — ₦5B cap). The template must launch in post_office mode first; campaign mode requires INEC Form CF001 reference gate.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'His Excellency [Name], President of the Federal Republic of Nigeria' (incumbent) or '[Name] for President 2027' (campaign). Reference: 'Aso Rock', 'State House', 'FEC', 'ECOWAS', 'AU', all 36 states + FCT, all 6 geopolitical zones (North-West, North-East, North-Central, South-West, South-East, South-South). INEC Form CF001 reference mandatory in campaign mode.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Presidential inauguration or campaign rally at Eagle Square Abuja. Running-mate 'ticket' duo portrait. 6-zone rally montage. Diaspora outreach event. Aso Rock Villa (incumbent). Presidential podium with Nigerian and state flags. High-production photography required — this is the highest-visibility political template.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC Form CF001 (presidential candidacy declaration). Party nomination certificate from presidential primary. INEC Certificate of Return (if elected). Campaign finance gate: no donation processing without INEC campaign account reference. Electoral Act 2022 ₦5B campaign spending cap disclosure. Tier 4 KYC for campaign mode with donation CTA.

notes: Sprint 3 P2. NF-POL-ELC standalone — presidential scope is unique, not a variant of governor. Highest revenue ceiling per tenant (₦500K–₦2M/month). Launch post_office first, then campaign mode with finance gate. Incumbent mode = Aso Rock official presence.

audienceSummary: Portfolio stakeholders, programme beneficiaries, and the general public seeking the appointee's role, mandate, programmes, and contact details. Journalists covering government appointments.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian political appointees cover a wide range of roles below commissioner level — Senior Special Assistants (SSA), Special Advisers (SA), Technical Advisers, Chief of Staff, Board Chairs of government agencies. This is a generalised template for appointed officials who do not have a specific role template. It is simpler than the commissioner template — no MDA hierarchy, reduced press briefing archive requirement.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title varies: 'Senior Special Assistant on [Portfolio]', 'Special Adviser on [Area]', 'Technical Adviser', etc. Reference appointing authority (governor or president by name). Use 'appointment letter date' as verifiable credential. No constituency language — appointed.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Appointee at government office. Meeting with supervisor (governor or minister). Programme implementation visit. Formal portrait in government setting. Nigerian state or federal government backdrop.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Appointment letter (gubernatorial or presidential). Tier 2 KYC: appointment letter upload. Oath of office if applicable.

nextAction: Blocked until state-commissioner anchor IMPLEMENTED. Pre-activate: add to CSV, confirm VN-ID.
notes: Sprint 3 P2. NF-POL-APT variant — simplified commissioner pattern for general appointees (SSAs, SAs, board chairs, chief of staff). No MDA hierarchy, no HASM screening. High volume across all 36 states.

audienceSummary: Ward residents seeking the councillor's contact, council session dates, ward development project updates, and constituency complaint process. The most localised political audience — often reached via WhatsApp before a website.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's ~8,809 elected ward councillors are the most numerous elected officials. They sit in the LGA council chamber (legislative arm), not the executive chair. The SIEC-issued Certificate of Return is the key credential. Ward boundary display and constituent WhatsApp CTA are essential features. Revenue per tenant is lower (₦5K–₦20K/month) but volume compensates. The template must be lightweight and mobile-first for low-bandwidth ward-level users.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable Councillor [Name], Ward [X], [LGA] Local Government Area'. Distinguish from LGA Chairman (executive). Reference: 'ward council chamber', 'council session', 'ward committee'. INEC ward code display. Party badge. WhatsApp is the primary constituent contact channel.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Councillor at LGA council chamber or ward office. Ward town hall in open community space (under a tree, in a school hall). Ward development project site. WhatsApp community group screenshot as trust signal. Very local Nigerian setting — no state house or Abuja.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: SIEC Certificate of Return. INEC ward boundary code. Tier 1–2 KYC: SIEC certificate upload. No INEC federal verification needed.

notes: Sprint 3 P2. NF-POL-ELC variant. Differentiation from vtx_ward_rep is critical: ward-councillor = SIEC-elected legislative representative with certificate of return; vtx_ward_rep = generic informal ward leader. Must be mobile-first and lightweight for 2G/3G ward-level usage.

audienceSummary: Party members at the ward or LGA chapter, prospective members, and community leaders engaging with the local party structure. Chapter officers are the grassroots face of Nigerian political parties.
businessContextSummary: Nigerian political parties (APC, PDP, LP, NNPP etc.) are organised from ward → LGA → state → zonal → national. Ward and LGA chapter officers are the frontline party structure — they run membership drives, mobilise voters, coordinate candidate campaigns at ward level, and maintain party discipline. INEC party registration applies at national level; ward/LGA chapters are governed by internal party rules. Tier 1 KYC only — party membership card is the credential.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: '[Name], [Chairman/Secretary/Youth Leader], [Party Acronym] [LGA/Ward] Chapter'. Reference party acronym prominently (APC, PDP, LP etc). Chapter officers include chairman, secretary, treasurer, youth leader, women leader. Party colours and logo are central. No INEC compliance language — party internal role.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Chapter officer at party secretariat (local level). Ward party meeting in open or enclosed community space. Party membership drive. Party primaries at ward level. Party colours prominently displayed on banners and t-shirts. Nigerian grassroots political energy.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Party membership card number. INEC party registration (national — reference only). Chapter appointment letter from state party secretariat. Tier 1 KYC: party card upload only.

notes: Sprint 3 P2. NF-POL-PTY family anchor. Must be IMPLEMENTED before party-state-officer can be claimed. Differentiation from vtx_political_party: vtx_political_party = the party organisation website; this = an individual chapter officer's personal profile site.

audienceSummary: Party members at state level, candidate aspirants seeking state party endorsement, journalists covering state party activities, and political analysts monitoring inter-party dynamics.
businessContextSummary: State-level party officers (State Chairman, State Secretary, State Women Leader, State Youth Leader, etc.) coordinate the party's activities across all LGA chapters in a state. They play a critical role in governorship primaries, candidate screening, and state campaign coordination. More visible than chapter officers — often appear in state media. INEC party registration applies at national level.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: '[Name], State [Chairman/Secretary/Women Leader], [Party Acronym] [State] State'. Reference 'State Working Committee' (SWC), 'State Executive Committee' (SEC party). Scope covers all LGA chapters in the state. Media coverage archive is an important feature at this level.
imageArtDirectionNotes: State party officer at state secretariat. State party congress. Press conference with party backdrop. State governorship primary coordination. Nigerian state capital setting.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: Party membership card. INEC party registration (national — reference only). State party congress minutes or appointment confirmation. Tier 1 KYC: party card upload.

audienceSummary: State residents monitoring the deputy governor's assigned portfolio (often women's development, SDGs, education, special duties). Journalists covering gubernatorial succession and running-mate dynamics.
businessContextSummary: Nigeria's 36 deputy governors are elected on joint ticket with their governor. In campaign mode, the deputy is the 'running mate' — co-featured on campaign materials. In incumbent mode, they hold a specific portfolio (Section 191 CFRN succession protocol applies — this is a constitutional reference). The deputy-governor template inherits the governor pattern but centres the joint-ticket dynamic and portfolio focus.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'His Excellency/Her Excellency [Name], Deputy Governor of [State] State'. Reference 'Joint Ticket', 'Running Mate' in campaign mode. Portfolio title in incumbent mode. Section 191 CFRN succession protocol as a constitutional trust signal.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Deputy governor duo portrait with governor (joint ticket). Portfolio programme implementation. State government setting. Campaign: duo rally appearance. Incumbent: portfolio sector event.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC joint-ticket Certificate of Return. Constitutional oath of office. Succession protocol reference (Section 191 CFRN). Tier 3 KYC for incumbent mode.

notes: Sprint 3 P2. NF-POL-ELC variant of governor. Joint ticket display and portfolio assignment are the key differentiators. Section 191 CFRN succession protocol is a constitutional trust reference unique to this niche.

verticalName: Speaker / Deputy Speaker, State House of Assembly
audienceSummary: State legislators, journalists covering state legislative proceedings, lobbyists, and civil society monitoring legislative agenda, speaker's rulings, and committee activities.
businessContextSummary: The Speaker of a State House of Assembly is an institutional presiding officer — elected by HASM peers. The role is distinct from an ordinary HASM: the speaker controls legislative procedure, committee assignments, and the house calendar. The institutional nature of the role (order papers, speaker's communiqués, committee-of-whole-house) requires a distinct presiding-officer mode separate from the standard campaign/incumbent split. Small market (36 speakers + 36 deputy speakers = 72) but high revenue per tenant.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Rt. Honourable [Name], Speaker, [State] State House of Assembly'. Reference 'Speaker's Office', 'House Committee', 'Order Paper', 'Rules of the House'. Presiding officer mode is institutional — not constituency-specific.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Speaker at podium in state HOA chamber with gavel. Speaker's official portrait in formal state HOA regalia. Committee of whole house session. Legislative calendar display.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: INEC Certificate of Return (for underlying constituency seat). State HOA peers' election resolution as speaker. Tier 2–3 KYC.
blockers: CSV row not yet added — niche not yet canonical

notes: Sprint 4 P2. NF-POL-ELC standalone — institutional presiding role distinct from ordinary HASM. Small market (72 accounts max) but high revenue per tenant. Requires 'presiding officer' mode concept in template.

audienceSummary: LGA residents monitoring the vice chairman's assigned portfolio (education, health, women affairs, environment at LGA level). Running-mate during campaign mode.
businessContextSummary: LGA vice chairmen are elected on joint ticket with the LGA chairman. In campaign mode, they are the running mate. In incumbent mode, they typically hold a committee or portfolio (education, health, women's development at LGA level). The succession protocol (vice chairman succeeds chairman) is a constitutional reference. Simplified variant of lga-chairman.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable Vice Chairman, [LGA Name] Local Government Area'. Reference joint ticket with chairman. Portfolio reference in incumbent mode. Succession protocol under LGA law.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Vice chairman with LGA chairman (joint ticket duo). Portfolio programme event. LGA council chamber. Campaign: joint rally appearance.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: SIEC joint-ticket Certificate of Return. LGA administrative code. Succession protocol under state local government law.

audienceSummary: LGA residents monitoring the supervisory councillor's portfolio (primary health, education, markets, environment at LGA level). Community stakeholders in the councillor's sector.
businessContextSummary: Supervisory councillors are appointed by the LGA chairman — they are the LGA equivalent of state commissioners. Each supervisory councillor oversees a specific LGA functional area (primary healthcare, markets, environment, education, sanitation, agriculture extension). Appointment is non-elective — no campaign mode. The simplest template in the NF-POL-APT family.
contentLocalizationNotes: Title: 'Honourable Supervisory Councillor for [Portfolio], [LGA Name] LGA'. Reference LGA functional area by name. Appointed by LGA chairman (name reference). No INEC language — appointed role.
imageArtDirectionNotes: Supervisory councillor at LGA secretariat or portfolio sector site (health centre, market stall, primary school). Community programme visit. LGA-level setting — very local Nigerian environment.
regulatoryOrTrustNotes: LGA chairman appointment letter. LGA administrative order. Tier 1 KYC: appointment letter upload. No SIEC or INEC verification needed.
